text.title: Strategy Analysis — Agentic Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: KA-6 router pattern, four sub-routine agent roles, prompt patterns for router and future-state validator, and LLM failure modes in strategy analysis.

rule.statement: A ka6-router-agent (sonnet) inspects which artifacts exist for an initiative and dispatches to the first missing or stale task. It refuses to advance to a later task if an earlier one is missing. It also refuses to reuse any artifact with last_validated_at older than 90 days.
rule.rationale: Skipping tasks silently or using stale artifacts are the two most common causes of strategy work that drives the wrong decisions.

example.note: KA-6 router prompt — strict JSON, sequential task enforcement, freshness gate.
example.code:
You are a BABOK KA-6 router. Inputs: - initiative_id - .aidocs/<initiative>/strategy/ directory listing with last_validated_at per artifact
Decide which of {current_state, future_state, risk, change_strategy} is missing or has last_validated_at > 90 days ago. Emit JSON: { next_task, reason, required_inputs[], blocking_gaps[] } Refuse to advance to a later task if an earlier one is missing. Refuse to reuse any artifact older than 90 days; require re-validation first.

example.note: Future-state schema validator — gate that rejects unmeasurable goals.
example.code: You are a future-state schema validator. For each goal in input JSON verify presence of: {metric, baseline, baseline_date, target, target_date, owner} Reject (status: "rejected", missing[]) if any field absent or if baseline == target. No prose output, only JSON. No exceptions.

list.item: current-state-agent (opus) — input: business need + org context. Output: current-state JSON across 6 areas; each row has evidence_url + interviewed[]

future-state-agent (opus) — input: business need + current-state + corporate strategy. Output: future-state JSON with goals (each: metric, baseline, baseline_date, target, target_date, owner). Paired with future-state-validator (sonnet) as gate
risk-agent (sonnet) — input: current + future state. Output: two risk registers (change risks AND do-nothing risks); each row: id, description, likelihood 1-5, impact 1-5, exposure, mitigation, owner

change-strategy-agent (opus) — input: current + future + risks + gap analysis. Output: strategy memo with N≥3 options including status-quo; recommended option with rationale. Hands off to decision-analysis methodology for the weighted matrix
faion-brainstorm — divergence pass before change-strategy-agent writes options; without it the option set collapses to whatever vendor decks were on the desk
faion-sdd-executor-agent — once change strategy is signed, scaffolds .aidocs/backlog/initiative/ with constitution → spec → design → implementation-plan stubs carrying strategy_id traces
faion-improver — quarterly meta-loop: rereads strategy artifacts older than 6 months, compares predictions vs observed metrics, updates .aidocs/memory/patterns.md
password-scrubber-agent — runs over every artifact before commit; strategy memos commonly leak vendor pricing, NDA-bound roadmaps, or named individuals

antipattern.description: Goal hallucination — future-state agent invents measurable-looking goals ("reduce cost 30%") with no source quote.
antipattern.reason: Require source_quote + source_doc per goal; reject any goal without a cited evidence span from an elicitation artifact.

antipattern.description: Technique laziness — SWOT and PESTLE chosen by default for every current-state.
antipattern.reason: Agent must justify the technique choice against the question being answered before applying it; document the choice in artifact metadata.

antipattern.description: Risk register monoculture — only "change risks" listed, no "do-nothing risks".
antipattern.reason: Encode both registers as required in the JSON schema; a validator rejects any risk output with only one register.

antipattern.description: Vendor-biased options — when the change-strategy-agent has access to vendor docs, options drift toward those vendors.
antipattern.reason: Force faion-brainstorm divergence first; options must include build and status-quo before any vendor option is considered.

antipattern.description: Sponsor-signature spoofing — agent sets signed_by field in the artifact.
antipattern.reason: The signed_by field is only writable by a human-driven sign-off step; agents must leave it null and halt for human checkpoint.

list.item: Sponsor signs the business need before current-state analysis begins
Exec sponsor locks the future state before risk assessment and strategy work
Decision-maker signs the change strategy before implementation planning
BA and faion-improver revisit predicted vs actual gap closure 6-12 months post-decision

text.reference: Bash gate that validates all four KA-6 JSON artifacts against schemas before commit
